mnemonics: COPY KIDS: Create Only Pages When Kids (processes) Demand Sharing
keyPoints: A memory management optimization where a parent and child process initially share the same physical memory pages.
A private copy of a memory page is created only when a process (parent or child) attempts to write to it.
Improves efficiency and reduces memory usage by deferring the copying of data until it is absolutely necessary.
commonMistakes: Assuming the copy is made immediately upon forking the process, rather than on the first write attempt.
Confusing it with traditional process creation where a full copy of the parent's address space is made immediately.
